scenario: Users in VLAN 10 report slow file shares. NOC notices traffic from SW1 to DSW1 is taking a non-optimal path. The distribution switch DSW1 should be root for VLAN 10, but it isn't.

initialView.output:

             Hello Time   2 sec  Max Age 20 sec  Forward Delay 15 sec
             Aging Time  300 sec

------------------- ---- --- --------- -------- --------------------------------
Gi0/1               Root FWD 4         128.25   P2p
Gi0/2               Altn BLK 4         128.26   P2p

AP-2F            Gig 0/24           135         T           AIR-CAP   Gig 0

question: Why is DSW1 not the root bridge for VLAN 10?

choices.text: DSW1 is unreachable — SW1 sees no CDP neighbor on Gi0/1
choices.explain: CDP output shows DSW1 is the neighbor on Gi0/1 — it is reachable.

choices.text: Some other switch with MAC aabb.cc00.0a01 has a lower priority (4106) than DSW1, making it root
choices.explain: Root ID priority is 4106 with MAC aabb.cc00.0a01 — that is not DSW1 (DSW1 should be lower than 4106 to win). A non-distribution switch has been configured with priority 4096 (4096+10=4106) and is winning the election.

choices.text: VLAN 10 is pruned from the trunk to DSW1
choices.explain: If VLAN 10 were pruned, SW1 wouldn't be receiving BPDUs and would think itself root.

choices.text: BPDU Guard is err-disabling the path to DSW1
choices.explain: BPDU Guard would shut the port; Gi0/1 is Root FWD (forwarding), so BPDUs are flowing fine.

rootCause: Root ID priority is 4106 (4096 + sys-id-ext 10) with MAC aabb.cc00.0a01. Distribution switch DSW1 should own root, but a different switch has been manually set with a lower priority and is winning the election.
fixHint: On DSW1: spanning-tree vlan 10 root primary  (or  spanning-tree vlan 10 priority 0)

title: BPDU Guard err-disabled an access port
scenario: A user in conference room 2F reports their laptop has no network. The access port Gi0/15 has been working fine for months. Cable looks plugged in but the switch port shows down.

initialView.output:
Port      Name               Status       Vlan       Duplex  Speed Type
Gi0/15    conf-room-2F       err-disabled 10         auto    auto 10/100/1000BaseTX

showCommands.show interfaces Gi0/15:
GigabitEthernet0/15 is down, line protocol is down (err-disabled)

  Description: conf-room-2F
  MTU 1500 bytes, BW 1000000 Kbit/sec, DLY 10 usec,
     reliability 255/255, txload 1/255, rxload 1/255
  Encapsulation ARPA, loopback not set
  Keepalive set (10 sec)
  Auto-duplex, Auto-speed, link type is auto, media type is 10/100/1000BaseTX
showCommands.show errdisable recovery:
ErrDisable Reason            Timer Status
-----------------            --------------
bpduguard                    Disabled
dtp-flap                     Disabled
link-flap                    Disabled
psecure-violation            Disabled
security-violation           Disabled
udld                         Disabled

Timer interval: 300 seconds

Interfaces that will be enabled at the next timeout:
(none)
showCommands.show logging | inc Gi0/15:
May 30 14:02:11.221: %SPANTREE-2-BLOCK_BPDUGUARD: Received BPDU on port Gi0/15 with BPDU Guard enabled. Disabling port.
May 30 14:02:11.225: %PM-4-ERR_DISABLE: bpduguard error detected on Gi0/15, putting Gi0/15 in err-disable state
May 30 14:02:13.110: %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/15, changed state to down
showCommands.show running-config interface Gi0/15:
interface GigabitEthernet0/15
 description conf-room-2F
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 10
 spanning-tree portfast
 spanning-tree bpduguard enable
end

question: What caused Gi0/15 to err-disable?

choices.text: Port-security MAC violation
choices.explain: Logs show bpduguard, not psecure-violation. No switchport port-security config on the interface.

choices.text: BPDU Guard tripped — a BPDU was received on a PortFast edge port
choices.explain: Log line %SPANTREE-2-BLOCK_BPDUGUARD shows a BPDU arrived on Gi0/15. The port is configured with portfast + bpduguard, so receiving any BPDU shuts it down. A user likely plugged in an unmanaged switch or another bridge.

choices.text: Cable is bad — physical layer failure
choices.explain: Status is err-disabled (administrative shutdown by IOS), not down/down. Cable is fine.

choices.text: VLAN 10 was deleted, leaving the port orphaned
choices.explain: If VLAN 10 were missing, the port would be inactive, not err-disabled.

rootCause: Log line %SPANTREE-2-BLOCK_BPDUGUARD confirms a BPDU arrived on Gi0/15. PortFast + BPDU Guard policy err-disables the port when any BPDU is received — likely a user plugged a hub/switch into the access port.
fixHint: Remove the rogue device, then: interface Gi0/15 / shutdown / no shutdown   (or enable errdisable recovery cause bpduguard)

question: Why can VLAN 30 hosts not reach DSW1?

choices.text: VLAN 30 does not exist on SW2
choices.explain: show vlan brief lists VLAN 30 GUEST active with assigned ports Gi0/14 and Gi0/15.

choices.text: Trunk encapsulation mismatch — DSW1 expects ISL
choices.explain: Operational Trunking Encapsulation is dot1q on both sides and the trunk is up.

choices.text: VLAN 30 is not in the trunk's allowed list (only 1,10,20,99 are allowed)
choices.explain: show interfaces trunk shows 'Vlans allowed on trunk: 1,10,20,99'. VLAN 30 is missing. The trunk drops VLAN 30 tagged frames.

choices.text: Native VLAN mismatch is blocking VLAN 30
choices.explain: Native VLAN affects untagged traffic only. VLAN 30 traffic is tagged, so native VLAN is not the issue.

rootCause: Trunk allowed list is 1,10,20,99. VLAN 30 is excluded, so guest traffic is dropped at the trunk. Hosts in VLAN 30 are isolated to SW2.
fixHint: interface Gi0/1 / switchport trunk allowed vlan add 30

question: What is the root cause of the syslog flood and untagged VLAN 99 issues?

choices.text: VLAN 99 is missing from the allowed list
choices.explain: Allowed list explicitly includes 99.

choices.text: Native VLAN is 99 on SW1 but 1 on SW2 — untagged frames land in different VLANs on each side
choices.explain: CDP log %CDP-4-NATIVE_VLAN_MISMATCH and 'show cdp neighbors detail' confirm SW2 native VLAN is 1 while SW1 is 99. Untagged frames sent by SW1 (in VLAN 99) are received into VLAN 1 by SW2.

choices.text: Trunk encapsulation is ISL on one side
choices.explain: Both sides show dot1q in the trunk encapsulation.

choices.text: STP is blocking VLAN 99 on Gi0/2
choices.explain: show spanning-tree vlan 99 shows Gi0/2 in Desg FWD state.

rootCause: CDP %CDP-4-NATIVE_VLAN_MISMATCH and CDP neighbor detail confirm SW1 native VLAN 99, SW2 native VLAN 1. Untagged management traffic leaks between VLANs.
fixHint: On SW2 Gi0/1: switchport trunk native vlan 99   (must match on both ends)

title: DTP mode mismatch (no trunk forms)
scenario: After a switch swap, the trunk between SW1 and SW3 is no longer carrying VLAN 20. Hosts on VLAN 20 attached to SW3 can only reach VLAN 1. Both interfaces look up/up.

initialView.output:

-------------------------

Operational Trunking Encapsulation: native

question: Why is the link an access port instead of a trunk?

choices.text: Trunk allowed list is empty
choices.explain: There is no operational trunk at all — 'show interfaces trunk' returns nothing.

choices.text: STP is blocking the trunk negotiation
choices.explain: STP does not influence DTP negotiation. The link is up and forwarding as access.

choices.text: SW1 is dynamic desirable but SW3 is static access — DTP cannot negotiate a trunk when one side is access
choices.explain: SW1 Admin Mode is dynamic desirable. SW3 Admin Mode is static access with Negotiation: Off. Two access/no-negotiate sides cannot form a trunk. Operational Mode on SW1 falls back to static access.

choices.text: Native VLAN mismatch is preventing the trunk
choices.explain: Native VLAN is the same (1) and only matters once a trunk forms.

rootCause: SW1 is dynamic desirable; SW3 is static access with negotiation off. DTP cannot pull SW3 into a trunk, so SW1 operationally falls back to access in VLAN 1.
fixHint: On SW3 Gi0/1: switchport mode trunk / switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q  (or set both ends to a deterministic trunk config)

title: Port-security sticky MAC violation
scenario: A user swapped laptops over the weekend. Monday morning they have no link. Port Gi0/10 shows err-disabled. The user insists 'nothing changed'.

initialView.output:
Port      Name               Status       Vlan       Duplex  Speed Type
Gi0/10    user-jdoe          err-disabled 10         auto    auto 10/100/1000BaseTX

Port Status                : Secure-shutdown
Violation Mode             : Shutdown
Aging Time                 : 0 mins
Aging Type                 : Absolute
SecureStatic Address Aging : Disabled
Maximum MAC Addresses      : 1
Total MAC Addresses        : 1
Configured MAC Addresses   : 0
Sticky MAC Addresses       : 1
Last Source Address:Vlan   : aabb.cc00.beef:10
Security Violation Count   : 1
showCommands.show running-config interface Gi0/10:
interface GigabitEthernet0/10
 description user-jdoe
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 10
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 1
 switchport port-security mac-address sticky
 switchport port-security mac-address sticky aabb.cc00.1234
 switchport port-security violation shutdown
 spanning-tree portfast
end
showCommands.show logging | inc Gi0/10:
Jun 01 08:12:44.225: %PORT_SECURITY-2-PSECURE_VIOLATION: Security violation occurred, caused by MAC address aabb.cc00.beef on port GigabitEthernet0/10.
Jun 01 08:12:44.230: %PM-4-ERR_DISABLE: psecure-violation error detected on Gi0/10, putting Gi0/10 in err-disable state
Jun 01 08:12:46.110: %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/10, changed state to down

question: Why is Gi0/10 err-disabled?

choices.text: BPDU Guard tripped — the new laptop is sending BPDUs
choices.explain: Log shows psecure-violation, not bpduguard. No bpduguard in running-config.

choices.text: STP put the port in blocking
choices.explain: err-disabled is not an STP state. The port is administratively shut by IOS.

choices.text: Sticky MAC pinned aabb.cc00.1234 to Gi0/10; the new laptop's MAC aabb.cc00.beef triggered a violation with violation mode shutdown
choices.explain: show port-security shows 'Last Source Address: aabb.cc00.beef' and 'Configured Sticky: aabb.cc00.1234'. Violation mode shutdown err-disables on mismatch. The 'nothing changed' was actually a laptop swap.

choices.text: VLAN 10 was removed and the port has no VLAN
choices.explain: Port still shows VLAN 10 in the status output.

rootCause: Sticky MAC aabb.cc00.1234 is locked to Gi0/10. New laptop MAC aabb.cc00.beef triggered %PORT_SECURITY-2-PSECURE_VIOLATION; violation mode shutdown err-disabled the port.
fixHint: interface Gi0/10 / no switchport port-security mac-address sticky aabb.cc00.1234 / shutdown / no shutdown  (or clear port-security sticky interface Gi0/10)

question: Why is the intern landing on the ENG subnet?

choices.text: VLAN 10 doesn't exist — DHCP fell back to VLAN 20
choices.explain: show vlan brief shows VLAN 10 SALES active.

choices.text: DHCP scope on the SALES server is exhausted, so the PC fell back to ENG
choices.explain: There is no fallback between scopes; DHCP requests don't cross VLANs without a relay decision. The port is simply in VLAN 20.

choices.text: Gi0/11 is configured as access VLAN 20 — the port itself is on ENG, not SALES, despite the patch panel label
choices.explain: show running-config interface Gi0/11 has 'switchport access vlan 20'. The MAC table and DHCP binding confirm traffic is in VLAN 20. Patch panel label is misleading; the config is the truth.

choices.text: Trunk allowed list excludes VLAN 10
choices.explain: This is an access port (not a trunk). Trunk allowed list does not apply.

rootCause: Interface Gi0/11 is hard-coded 'switchport access vlan 20' even though the patch panel label says VLAN 10. MAC table and DHCP binding confirm the host is in VLAN 20.

showCommands.show spanning-tree vlan 30: Spanning tree instance(s) for vlan 30 does not exist.

question: Why are guest clients not appearing on VLAN 30?

choices.text: VLAN 30 doesn't exist on SW2
choices.explain: show vlan brief lists VLAN 30 GUEST as active.

choices.text: Trunk is blocking VLAN 30
choices.explain: Trunk allowed list includes 30. (STP doesn't forward 30 only because no access port is using it locally — symptom, not cause.)

choices.text: The WLC port Gi0/14 has no 'switchport access vlan 30' — it defaulted to VLAN 1. VLAN 30 has no member ports.
choices.explain: show vlan brief: VLAN 30 row has no listed ports. show interfaces Gi0/14 status shows the WLC sitting in VLAN 1. Running-config confirms 'switchport mode access' but no 'switchport access vlan 30' command. The intent never made it to the port.

choices.text: VTP pruning removed VLAN 30
choices.explain: VLAN 30 is still active in the database; pruning would just stop flooding across the trunk, not remove the entry.

rootCause: VLAN 30 row in show vlan brief has no member ports. WLC port Gi0/14 is access but missing the 'switchport access vlan 30' line and defaulted to VLAN 1.

title: EtherChannel mode mismatch (LACP vs PAgP)
scenario: Po1 between SW1 and DSW1 is supposed to be a 2x1G LACP bundle. After a maintenance window, Po1 is down. Both physical links are up/up individually.

initialView.output:
Flags:  D - down        P - bundled in port-channel
        I - stand-alone s - suspended
        H - Hot-standby (LACP only)
        R - Layer3      S - Layer2
        U - in use      f - failed to allocate aggregator
        M - not in use, minimum links not met
        u - unsuitable for bundling
        w - waiting to be aggregated
        d - default port

Number of channel-groups in use: 1
Number of aggregators:           1

Group  Port-channel  Protocol    Ports
------+-------------+-----------+-----------------------------------------------
1      Po1(SD)         LACP      Gi0/23(I)  Gi0/24(I)

showCommands.show running-config interface Gi0/23:
interface GigabitEthernet0/23
 description po1-member-to-DSW1
 switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
 switchport mode trunk
 channel-group 1 mode active
end
showCommands.DSW1# show running-config interface Gi1/:
interface GigabitEthernet1/0/23
 description po1-member-to-SW1
 switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
 switchport mode trunk
 channel-group 1 mode desirable
end
showCommands.show logging | inc EC-5:
May 30 11:02:14.221: %EC-5-CANNOT_BUNDLE2: Gi0/23 is not compatible with Po1 and will be suspended (flag mismatch).
May 30 11:02:14.225: %EC-5-CANNOT_BUNDLE2: Gi0/24 is not compatible with Po1 and will be suspended (flag mismatch).

question: Why is Po1 down?

choices.text: VLAN 1 is not in the trunk allowed list
choices.explain: Trunk allowed list is not the issue — the bundle never forms in the first place, so allowed list is moot.

choices.text: SW1 is using LACP (channel-group mode active); DSW1 is using PAgP (channel-group mode desirable). Protocols cannot mix — neither side bundles.
choices.explain: SW1 'show etherchannel summary' shows Protocol LACP, DSW1 shows Protocol PAgP. Running-configs confirm 'mode active' (LACP) vs 'mode desirable' (PAgP). The two protocols are incompatible. Logs show %EC-5-CANNOT_BUNDLE2.

choices.text: STP is blocking both member ports
choices.explain: Member ports show (I) standalone, not blocking. The bundle itself never formed.

choices.text: MTU mismatch between the two ports
choices.explain: No MTU configuration is shown in running-config — both default to 1500.

rootCause: SW1 uses LACP (channel-group mode active); DSW1 uses PAgP (mode desirable). The two protocols are incompatible and members suspend with %EC-5-CANNOT_BUNDLE2.
fixHint: On DSW1: interface range Gi1/0/23-24 / no channel-group 1 mode desirable / channel-group 1 mode active   (match LACP on both sides)

title: MAC flapping — Layer 2 loop between two ports

question: What is the root cause of the MAC flapping and outages?

choices.text: STP is broken — both ports should be blocking
choices.explain: STP is healthy on this switch; the issue is that both Gi0/5 and Gi0/6 are PortFast edge ports with no BPDU Guard, so a downstream loop is not being detected as a topology change.

choices.text: Port-security is moving MACs between ports
choices.explain: No port-security on either interface.

choices.text: A cable or unmanaged hub bridges Gi0/5 and Gi0/6 downstream, creating a Layer 2 loop. Frames return to SW1 on both ports — MACs flap and broadcasts multiply (high mcast/bcast counters).
choices.explain: %SW_MATM-4-MACFLAP_NOTIF logs show multiple MACs flapping between Gi0/5 and Gi0/6. Multicast/broadcast counters on Gi0/5 are abnormally high. Both ports are PortFast edge with no BPDU Guard, so a downstream hub or patch loop is invisible to STP.

choices.text: VLAN 10 is missing on the trunk
choices.explain: These are access ports; trunk allowed list is irrelevant. The flap is between two local access ports.

rootCause: %SW_MATM-4-MACFLAP_NOTIF logs show host MACs flapping between Gi0/5 and Gi0/6. Both ports are PortFast access with no BPDU Guard — a downstream hub or duplicate patch cord is bridging them, creating a Layer 2 loop.
fixHint: Locate the rogue patch / unmanaged switch and remove it. Add: spanning-tree bpduguard enable on all access ports (and consider spanning-tree loopguard / storm-control).
